cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the text again and find the relevant information to answer the question. The question is about a search group formed by American scientists and what they did. The text mentions that in 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter long body and a very ugly head on the end of a four-meter neck. This led many people to believe in the monster. However, it's also noted that even today we can't be certain. Looking at the choices: (A) found the monster itself – The text doesn't say they found the monster, just that some pictures seemed to show a monster, so this isn't correct. (B) found a huge cave under water – This was mentioned earlier in the text, but that was from a different attempt, not the American scientists' group in 1975. (C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all – The text doesn't state their belief, just that they took pictures which some interpreted as a monster. (D) took some pictures which seemed to show a monster – This matches the text's description
